reasoning: Nvidia's price is currently around $188 as of early April 2026, meaning it would require a ~47% drawdown to drop below $100 natively. Over the remaining ~8.5 months of the year, standard Geometric Brownian Motion with a typical tech volatility of 45-50% gives a hitting probability of roughly 10% for this barrier. Furthermore, Q1 2026 TSMC revenue numbers were exceptionally strong (+35% YoY), and Nvidia itself provided massive quarterly revenue guidance of $65B, suggesting the fundamental AI capex story remains highly intact for now. While macroeconomic factors and geopolitical tensions in early 2026 pose ongoing risks, a 47% collapse by year-end is improbable barring a Black Swan event (like a sudden Taiwan invasion or a complete AI capex freeze). The community prediction was 0.12 in early April; with the stock recently rallying to $188, the barrier is slightly further away, leading me to estimate an even lower probability of 0.09 for the remainder of 2026. Note: the 3 probabilities provided represent the requested N, /, A dates, incremented slightly as requested by the horizon instructions.
